post.description:
Accepts a certificate request and starts its processing.

Allowed private key types:
- RSA (OID: 1.2.840.113549.1.1.1), key length equal to 2048 bits,
- EC (elliptic curve based keys, OID: 1.2.840.10045.2.1), NIST P-256 curve (secp256r1)

EC keys are recommended.

Allowed signature algorithms:
- RSA PKCS#1 v1.5,
- RSA PSS,
- ECDSA (signature format compliant with RFC 3279)

Allowed hash functions used for CSR signing:
- SHA1,
- SHA256,
- SHA384,
- SHA512

> More information:
> - [Sending certificate request](https://github.com/CIRFMF/ksef-docs/blob/main/certyfikaty-KSeF.md#4-wys%C5%82anie-wniosku-certyfikacyjnego)

post.summary: Grant permissions to natural persons to work in KSeF
post.description:
The method allows granting permissions to work in KSeF to the person specified in the request in the current context.
            
The request specifies the permissions to be granted from the set:  
- **InvoiceWrite** – issuing invoices,  
- **InvoiceRead** – viewing invoices,  
- **CredentialsManage** – managing permissions,  
- **CredentialsRead** – viewing permissions,  
- **Introspection** – viewing session history and generating UPO,  
- **SubunitManage** – managing subordinate units,  
- **EnforcementOperations** – performing enforcement operations.
            
The method allows selecting any combination of the above permissions.  
The **EnforcementOperations** permission can only be granted when the context subject has the role **EnforcementAuthority** (enforcement authority) or **CourtBailiff** (court bailiff).

> More information:
> - [Granting permissions](https://github.com/CIRFMF/ksef-docs/blob/main/uprawnienia.md#nadawanie-uprawnie%C5%84-osobom-fizycznym-do-pracy-w-ksef)

**Required permissions**: `CredentialsManage`.

post.description:
The method allows granting permissions for invoice handling of the context subject to the entity specified in the request.  
The request specifies the permissions to be granted from the set:  
- **InvoiceWrite** – issuing invoices  
- **InvoiceRead** – viewing invoices  
            
The method allows selecting any combination of the above permissions.  
For each permission, a **canDelegate** flag can be set, indicating the possibility of its further transfer through indirect granting.

> More information:
> - [Granting permissions](https://github.com/CIRFMF/ksef-docs/blob/main/uprawnienia.md#nadanie-podmiotom-uprawnie%C5%84-do-obs%C5%82ugi-faktur)

post.description:
The method allows granting permissions indirectly to the person specified in the request for handling invoices of another entity – client.  
This can be one of the possibilities:  
- granting general permissions – for handling all clients  
- granting selective permissions – for handling the specified client  
            
Selective permission can be granted only when the client has previously granted the entity of the current context any permission with the right to further transfer it (see [POST /v2/permissions/entities/grants](/docs/v2/index.html#tag/Nadawanie-uprawnien/paths/~1permissions~1entities~1grants/post)).  
            
The request specifies the permissions to be granted from the set:  
- **InvoiceWrite** – invoice issuing  
- **InvoiceRead** – invoice viewing  
            
The method allows selecting any combination of the above permissions.

> More information:
> - [Granting permissions](https://github.com/CIRFMF/ksef-docs/blob/main/uprawnienia.md#nadanie-uprawnie%C5%84-w-spos%C3%B3b-po%C5%9Bredni)

**Required permissions**: `CredentialsManage`.

post.description:
The method allows granting administrator permissions to the entity or natural person specified in the request in the context of:  
- the specified NIP of the subordinate entity – only if the entity of the current login context has the role of a parent entity:
  - **LocalGovernmentUnit** 
  - **VatGroupUnit**  
- the specified or created internal identifier  
            
Along with creating the administrator of the subordinate unit, an internal identifier is created consisting of the NIP number of the login context entity and 5 digits uniquely identifying the internal unit.
The last digit must be a correct control sum, which is calculated according to the algorithm below.

The algorithm uses alternating weights (1×, 3×, 1×, 3×, ...), sums the results and returns the remainder from division by 10.

Example:
- Input: "6824515772-1234" (without control digit)
- Position 0 (1st digit): 6 × 1 = 6
- Position 1 (2nd digit): 8 × 3 = 24
- Position 2 (3rd digit): 2 × 1 = 2
- Position 3 (4th digit): 4 × 3 = 12
- Position 4 (5th digit): 5 × 1 = 5
- Position 5 (6th digit): 1 × 3 = 3
- Position 6 (7th digit): 5 × 1 = 5
- Position 7 (8th digit): 7 × 3 = 21
- Position 8 (9th digit): 7 × 1 = 7
- Position 9 (10th digit): 2 × 3 = 6
- Position 10 (11th digit): 1 × 1 = 1
- Position 11 (12th digit): 2 × 3 = 6
- Position 12 (13th digit): 3 × 1 = 3
- Position 13 (14th digit): 4 × 3 = 12
- Sum: 6 + 24 + 2 + 12 + 5 + 3 + 5 + 21 + 7 + 6 + 1 + 6 + 3 + 12 = 113
- Control digit (15th digit): 113 % 10 = 3

The request also specifies the name of this unit.  
            
The subordinate unit administrator permissions include:  
- **CredentialsManage** – permissions management  
            
The method automatically grants the above permission, without the need to specify it in the request.
            
> More information:
> - [Granting permissions](https://github.com/CIRFMF/ksef-docs/blob/main/uprawnienia.md#nadanie-uprawnie%C5%84-administratora-podmiotu-podrz%C4%99dnego)

**Required permissions**: `SubunitManage`.

post.description:
The method allows granting administrator permissions to the entity or natural person specified in the request in a composite context consisting of the NIP identifier of the current context entity and the EU VAT number of the EU entity specified in the request.  
Along with creating the EU entity administrator, a composite context is created consisting of the NIP number of the login context entity and the specified EU VAT identification number of the EU entity.  
The request also specifies the name and address of the EU entity.  
            
The only way to identify the authorized party is the qualified certificate fingerprint:  
- electronic signature certificate for natural persons  
- electronic seal certificate for entities  
            
EU entity administrator permissions include:  
- **VatEuManage** – permissions management within the EU entity  
- **InvoiceWrite** – invoice issuing  
- **InvoiceRead** – invoice viewing  
- **Introspection** – session history viewing  
            
The method automatically grants all the above permissions, without the need to specify them in the request.
            
> More information:
> - [Granting permissions](https://github.com/CIRFMF/ksef-docs/blob/main/uprawnienia.md#nadanie-uprawnie%C5%84-administratora-podmiotu-unijnego)

**Required permissions**: `CredentialsManage`.

post.description: Starts an asynchronous process of searching for invoices in the KSeF system based on the provided filters and preparing them in the form of an encrypted package. It is required to provide encryption information in the Encryption field, which is used to secure the prepared invoice package. A maximum of 10 concurrent exports can be running in the logged-in context. The system retrieves invoices in ascending order by the date specified in the filter (Invoicing, Issue, PermanentStorage) and adds invoices (file name: {ksefNumber}.xml) to the package until one of the following limits is reached: * Invoice count limit: 10,000 pieces * Data size limit (compressed): 1GB The export package contains an additional file with invoice metadata in JSON format (`_metadata.json`). The file content is an object with an invoices array, where each element is an object of type InvoiceMetadata (same as returned by the `POST /invoices/query/metadata` endpoint). The metadata file (_metadata.json) is not counted towards the package building algorithm limits. `For incremental retrieval, use filtering by PermanentStorage date`. **Sorting:** - permanentStorageDate | invoicingDate | issueDate (Asc) - field selected based on filters **Required permissions**: `InvoiceRead`.

post.description:
This method allows reading own permissions of the authenticated API client in the current login context.  

 The response includes the following permissions:  
 - granted directly in the current context  
 - granted by a parent entity  
 - granted indirectly, if the context login entity is an intermediary or target entity in the permission  
 - granted to an entity for invoice handling by another entity, if the authenticated entity has ownership permissions in the current context  

 Permissions returned by the operation include:  
 - **CredentialsManage** – permission management  
 - **CredentialsRead** – permission viewing  
 - **InvoiceWrite** – invoice issuing  
 - **InvoiceRead** – invoice viewing  
 - **Introspection** – session history viewing  
 - **SubunitManage** – subordinate entity management  
 - **EnforcementOperations** – enforcement operations execution  
 - **VatEuManage** – permission management within EU entity

post.summary: Get list of permissions granted to natural persons or entities to work in KSeF
post.description:
Method allows reading permissions granted to a natural person or entity.  
 The list of retrieved permissions can be of two types:  
 - List of all permissions valid in the current login context (used when an administrator wants to review permissions of all users in the current context)  
 - List of all permissions granted in the current context by the authenticated API client (used when an administrator wants to review the list of permissions they granted in the current context)  

 For the first list (valid permissions) the response includes:  
 - persons and entities that can work in the current context except for persons authorized indirectly  
 - persons authorized indirectly through the entity of the current context  

 For the second list (granted permissions) the response includes:  
 - permissions granted directly to work in the current context or in subordinate unit contexts  
 - permissions granted indirectly to handle clients of the current context entity  

 Permissions returned by the operation include:  
 - **CredentialsManage** – permission management  
 - **CredentialsRead** – permission viewing  
 - **InvoiceWrite** – invoice issuance  
 - **InvoiceRead** – invoice viewing  
 - **Introspection** – session history viewing  
 - **SubunitManage** – subordinate entity management  
 - **EnforcementOperations** – enforcement operations execution  

 Response can be filtered based on parameters:  
 - **authorIdentifier** – identifier of the person who granted the permission  
 - **authorizedIdentifier** – identifier of the authorized person or entity  
 - **targetIdentifier** – identifier of the target entity for indirectly granted permissions  
 - **permissionTypes** – list of searched permission types  
 - **permissionState** – permission status  
 - **queryType** – query type specifying which of the two lists should be returned  

#### Result pagination
The query returns **one page of results** with the number and size specified in the path.
- On the first call, set the parameter `pageOffset = 0`.  
- If the next page of results is available, the **`hasMore`** flag will appear in the response.  
- In such case, you can call the query again with the next page number.

 > More information:
 > - [Retrieving permissions list](https://github.com/CIRFMF/ksef-docs/blob/main/uprawnienia.md#pobranie-listy-uprawnie%C5%84-do-pracy-w-ksef-nadanych-osobom-fizycznym-lub-podmiotom)

**Sorting:**

- startDate (Desc)
- id (Asc)



**Required permissions**: `CredentialsManage`, `CredentialsRead`, `SubunitManage`.

post.summary: Retrieve list of permissions for administrators or representatives of EU entities authorized for self-invoicing
post.description:
This method allows reading permissions for administrators or representatives of EU entities:  
 - If the login context is NIP, it is possible to read permissions for administrators of EU entities associated with the entity of the current context, i.e., those for which the first part of the composite context equals the NIP of the login context.  
 - If the login context is composite (NIP-VAT EU), it is possible to retrieve all permissions for administrators and representatives of the entity in the current composite context.  

 Permissions returned by the operation include:  
 - **VatUeManage** – managing permissions within the EU entity  
 - **InvoiceWrite** – issuing invoices  
 - **InvoiceRead** – viewing invoices  
 - **Introspection** – viewing session history  

 The response can be filtered based on the following parameters:  
 - **vatUeIdentifier** – EU entity identifier  
 - **authorizedFingerprintIdentifier** – certificate fingerprint of the authorized person or entity  
 - **permissionTypes** – list of permission types to search for  

#### Results pagination
The query returns **one page of results** with the page number and size specified in the path.
- On the first call, set the parameter `pageOffset = 0`.  
- If another page of results is available, a **`hasMore`** flag will appear in the response.  
- In such case, you can call the query again with the next page number.
 
 > More information:
 > - [Retrieving permissions list](https://github.com/CIRFMF/ksef-docs/blob/main/uprawnienia.md#pobranie-listy-uprawnie%C5%84-administrator%C3%B3w-lub-reprezentant%C3%B3w-podmiot%C3%B3w-unijnych-uprawnionych-do-samofakturowania)

**Sorting:**

- startDate (Desc)
- id (Asc)



**Required permissions**: `CredentialsManage`, `CredentialsRead`, `VatUeManage`.

permanentStorageHwmDate.description: Applies only to queries filtered by date type PermanentStorage. If the query concerned the most recent period, this value may be slightly adjusted relative to the upper limit specified in the query conditions. For older periods, it will be consistent with the query conditions. The system guarantees that data below this value is consistent and complete. Repeated queries covering data below this moving timestamp will not return different results in the future (e.g., additional invoices). For dateType = Issue or Invoicing – null.

restrictToPermanentStorageHwmDate.description:
Determines whether the system should restrict filtering (dateRange.to range) to the `PermanentStorageHwmDate` value.

* Applies only to queries with `dateType = PermanentStorage`,

* When `null` or `false`, filtering may extend beyond `PermanentStorageHwmDate`.

dateRange.description:
The type and range of dates by which invoices are filtered.
Maximum allowed period is 3 months in UTC or Europe/Warsaw (WAW) time zone.
            
Date format:
 * Dates must be provided in ISO 8601 format, e.g., `yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss`.
 * The following variants are allowed:
   - with `Z` suffix (UTC time),
   - with explicit offset, e.g., `+01:00`, `+03:00`,
   - without offset (interpreted as local time in Europe/Warsaw time zone).

batchFile.description: Information about the invoice batch file being sent.

encryption.description: Symmetric encryption key for the batch file, encrypted with the Ministry of Finance public key.

offlineMode.description: Specifies whether the taxpayer declares "offline" invoicing mode for documents sent in the batch session.

partUploadRequests.description: Data required for proper upload of individual parts of the invoice batch file. Each file part declared in fileParts must be sent according to its corresponding object in partUploadRequests. The link between the declaration and the upload instruction is the ordinalNumber field. For each part: * apply the HTTP method specified in method, * set the address from url, * attach headers from headers, * attach the file part content in the request body. `Note: do not add the access token (accessToken) to the headers.` Each part is sent with a separate HTTP request. Returned response codes: * 201 – successful file acceptance, * 400 – invalid data, * 401 – incorrect authentication, * 403 – insufficient write permissions (e.g., upload time expired).

tags.name: Certyfikaty
tags.description:
KSeF Certificate is a digital identity certificate issued by the KSeF system upon request from an authenticated entity. 
This certificate can be used for:

- authentication in the KSeF system,
- performing operations in offline mode, including issuing invoices directly in the user's application.

**Note**: Applying for a KSeF Certificate is only possible after authentication using a signature (XAdES). Authentication using a KSeF system token does not allow submitting an application.

tags.name: Dane testowe
tags.description:
API for creating and managing test data, such as entities, natural persons and permissions. Possible entities to create are: enforcement authority, VAT group and local government units. For natural persons, you can specify whether the person is a bailiff. The functionality of granting and receiving permissions is intended to reflect the operation of the ZAW-FA form in the test environment.

More information:
- [Test scenarios](https://github.com/CIRFMF/ksef-docs/blob/main/dane-testowe-scenariusze.md)

tags.name: Tokeny KSeF
tags.description:
KSeF Token is a unique, generated authentication identifier that — along with [qualified electronic signature](https://github.com/CIRFMF/ksef-docs/blob/main/uwierzytelnianie.md#21-uwierzytelnianie-kwalifikowanym-podpisem-elektronicznym) — enables [authentication](https://github.com/CIRFMF/ksef-docs/blob/main/uwierzytelnianie.md#22-uwierzytelnianie-tokenem-ksef) to the KSeF API. KSeF Token is issued with an immutable set of permissions specified during its creation; any modification of these permissions requires generating a new token. > **Warning!**
> KSeF Token serves as a **confidential authentication secret** — it should be stored only in a trusted and secure repository. More information: - [KSeF Token](https://github.com/CIRFMF/ksef-docs/blob/main/tokeny-ksef.md)

tags.name: Uzyskiwanie dostępu
tags.description:
Authentication in the KSeF API 2.0 system is a mandatory step that must be performed before accessing protected system resources. This process is based on **obtaining an access token** (```accessToken```) in ```JWT``` format, which is then used to authorize API operations.

> More information:
> - [Authentication](https://github.com/CIRFMF/ksef-docs/blob/main/uwierzytelnianie.md)
